title: Mass casualty first thirty minutes board
summary: Organizes the first half hour after an event with many injured people when professional help is delayed.
content: In a mass casualty event, chaos kills through duplicated effort, blocked exits, missed breathing problems, and missing information. Create one visible board with scene dangers, helper roles, casualty count, urgent transport names, supply needs, and time of last check. The board does not diagnose; it keeps people from disappearing into the crowd.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
paper and pen. Context: “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Mass casualty first thirty minutes board»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Mass casualty first thirty minutes board»
steps: Choose one calm coordinator and one recorder.
Mark unsafe zones, exits, and a quiet casualty collection point.
Record red flags first: breathing trouble, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe burns, crush injury, pregnancy danger, infant distress.
Send runners for help, water, lights, blankets, and transport.
Recheck every listed person on a fixed schedule.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Mass casualty first thirty minutes board” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Mass casualty first thirty minutes board.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Mass casualty first thirty minutes board.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Mass casualty first thirty minutes board.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Mass casualty first thirty minutes board.
